directive: Rebuild angle 0 around the same field-tier CAR-censored exact-initiation-time kernel and the same honest standard scope—interior u_star, one initiation, known s in (0,2] and L, local initiation positivity, consistency/sequential exchangeability as the explicit content of causal validity, CAR censoring, and uniformly bounded hazards—without adding an assumption that asserts identification, the EIF bridge, the minimax rate, Gaussian coupling, or the lower bound. First separate the full-data causal law from the observed law: define from the observed transition/initiation/censoring hazards a displayed g-formula or product-integral Psi_u(P), state precisely the standard consistency, sequential-randomization/causal-validity, positivity, and CAR conditions under which P_full(T^u <= t_0)=Psi_u(P), explain that u maps to Psi_u(P) is initially identified only f_U-a.e., and prove that membership in the stated Hölder class yields a unique continuous/Hölder version and hence the observed-law target theta(P)=Psi_{u_star}(P). Next define an actual stochastic initiation intervention on the at-risk counting-process hazard, including eligibility, its conditional initiation density/hazard, survival/no-initiation mass, support, normalization, and positivity domination, and prove that its identified risk equals the proposed regularized functional. Resolve the kernel-sign problem explicitly: either use a nonnegative normalized symmetric kernel sufficient for s<=2, or represent a signed local-polynomial equivalent kernel as a declared signed linear combination of legitimate stochastic-intervention risks and derive its EIF by pathwise linearity; never call a signed kernel an intervention density. Write the Martinussen–Knudsen–Rytgaard EIF with all nuisance arguments and prove a bridge proposition showing that it is the EIF of exactly this theta_b(P), rather than claiming that “insertion” works. Define v_{P,b}(c)=Var_P{phi_{P,b(c)}(O)} asymptotic to b(c)^(-1), s_{P,n}(c)=sqrt(v_{P,b(c)}(c)/n) asymptotic to {n b(c)}^(-1/2), and s_hat_n(c)=sqrt(P_n[phi_hat_c-P_n phi_hat_c]^2/n); use s_hat_n, not the raw score standard deviation, in every studentized process, bias ratio, and interval, and require/display uniform relative variance consistency. Give the cross-fitted estimator and a uniform expansion theta_hat_b(c)-theta_b(c)=(P_n-P)phi_{P,b(c)}+R_{1n}(c)+R_{2n}(c), identifying every remainder and its localization powers. Replace the circular complexity condition with checkable CCK-style predicates: define the standardized triangular class F_n={sqrt(b(c)) phi_{P,b(c)}/sqrt(b(c)v_{P,b(c)}):c in C}, its envelope, canonical covariance metric, and entropy metric; state a fixed q>=4 envelope/moment bound, a uniform VC-type or explicit covering bound sup_Q log N(epsilon ||F_n||_{Q,2},F_n,L_2(Q)) <= v_n log(A_n/epsilon), uniform nondegenerate variance and covariance-estimation bounds, the exact vanishing CCK coupling expression from the cited theorem specialized to m_n=n inf_c b_n(c) (including every logarithmic and q-moment term), Gaussian sample boundedness/equicontinuity, an explicit anti-concentration inequality for sup_c |G_n(c)|, and studentization plus nuisance/Bahadur remainders small enough relative to that anti-concentration scale. State the nuisance conditions component by component in the actual localized norms, including score-estimation and second-order product terms, rather than an unnamed sum; either exhibit a concrete verifiable regime—such as correctly specified bounded finite-dimensional or explicitly dimension-controlled sieve hazard, censoring, and delayed-entry survival estimators—with calculations proving all rates, or label the result honestly conditional on those displayed high-level rates and state that fully adaptive delayed-entry survival learning remains unresolved. Formulate the minimax lower bound over observed-law functionals theta(P), construct or clearly specify a nonempty CAR-preserving bounded-hazard local submodel (absence of censoring is permissible as a lower-bound subclass), verify positivity, Hölder membership, target separation h^s, and KL/Hellinger order n h^(2s+1), and do not place estimator-specific learnability or Gaussian-coupling assumptions in the lower-bound class unless the chosen subclass independently verifies them. Define the Hölder convention and kernel moment convention so the displayed worst-case bias constant is correct; distinguish fixed-c from simultaneous-c coverage; state the honest-coverage quantifiers and derive any expected-length lower bound from an explicit modulus/testing argument. Relabel the rate-optimal upper bound, matching converse, Gaussian approximation, honest CI, and optimal-length lower bound collectively and individually as CONJECTURES; retain only genuinely proved identification, intervention-equivalence, EIF-linearity, bias, or reduction statements as lemmas/propositions, and leave the primitive necessary-and-sufficient Gaussian frontier as an open question rather than assuming its answer. Finally repair the novelty chain wholesale: add Bonvini–Kennedy and Kennedy–Ma–McHugh–Small, cover every closest continuous-treatment, concentrating-intervention, survival-EIF, CCK, and honest-CI comparator, give verified theorem/section/equation/page locators for every claimed gap, state precisely what censoring/multistate/shrinking-window feature is absent from each comparator, and make every promise-table entry agree with a formal statement—either add a proved fixed-window reduction/strict-extension proposition relative to Martinussen–Knudsen–Rytgaard or downgrade that relation to “informed by.” Preserve the 1,896-couple IUI analysis only as the applied consumer and do not claim exact-day causal identification or valid inference there beyond the reconstructed assumptions and conjectural status.

--angle-action continue. Rebuild angle 0 v5 wholesale around one focal open problem—the effective-count phase transition for the CAR-censored illness-death EIF—and incorporate every remaining defect in a single coherent specification. First, replace ass:sequential-exchangeability and its Martinussen–Knudsen–Rytgaard attribution with the source’s honestly stated observed-hazard causal-validity premise, explicitly distinguishing the full-filtration causal intervention from the observed-filtration intervention and disclosing that their correspondence is assumed rather than proved; if the displayed full-versus-observed counterfactual-intensity equalities are retained, remove the “standard sequential randomization” citation, tag them as novel/unverified identifying restrictions, explain their substantive credibility, and do not present them as published substrate. Keep exact-time identification and the localized-intervention/EIF bridge as non-novel substrate and prove any bridge actually used. Second, delete the assertion that sigma_P^2/m_n is a “sharp regular asymptotic variance frontier” merely because R_n is defined to have influence function phi_{P,b_n}. Prefer removing that efficiency clause entirely. If efficiency remains as a separate open question, define the comparison class independently as all estimators locally regular under explicit contiguous parametric or LAN triangular alternatives through each P, state the local parameter and loss, and ask for a Le Cam/convolution or local-asymptotic-minimax lower bound as a genuine proof; do not define admissibility by already imposing the candidate EIF expansion. Third, retain the substantive phase kernel but state it as an open construction: for n b_n→∞, derive—not assume—a pointwise and, only if supportable, uniform Lindeberg/Gaussian result from primitive EIF-envelope, conditional-variance, CAR-weight, and cross-fitted remainder calculations; for n b_n→lambda in (0,∞), construct an explicit nonempty CAR-preserving bounded-hazard triangular subclass inside the existing scope and prove convergence of the localized initiation point process and its marks by Laplace functionals or an equivalent rare-event theorem, identify the centered marked compound-Poisson law including its intensity, mark distribution, compensator, and any atom, show all nonlocal EIF and nuisance terms vanish at the critical scale, and prove the limit is non-Gaussian. State expressly that the compound-Poisson premise is not a definitional consequence of m_n or def:critical-law-handle; def:critical-law-handle may name only a candidate law until this construction is completed. Do not add an assumption saying that the marked process converges to Poisson, that a Gaussian coupling holds, or any equivalent condition encoding the desired phase result. Fourth, replace oeq:exact-target-implication with a named exact-target honest-inference focal object. Define the fixed model P_s(L) under the accepted interior illness-death scope; define C_{n,alpha} as all measurable confidence procedures C_n satisfying liminf_n inf_{P in P_s(L)} P^n{theta(P) in C_n}≥1-alpha; define diameter loss, for example D_n(C_n)=sup_{P in P_s(L)} E_P[diam(C_n)], and the honest benchmark D^*_{n,alpha}=inf_{C_n in C_{n,alpha}}D_n(C_n). Give the candidate bias-aware interval C^{BA}_{n,alpha}(b) centered at the cross-fitted theta_hat_b, with estimated standard error {v_hat_b/n}^{1/2}, the explicit worst-case Hölder bias bound B_s(b), and a bias-to-standard-error critical value (or another fully specified honest construction). Then define a formal bandwidth set such as B^G_alpha={(b_n):b_n↓0, n b_n→∞, C^{BA}_{n,alpha}(b_n) in C_{n,alpha}}, together with an optimal subset or threshold defined by minimizing D_n or comparing it with D^*_{n,alpha}; ask whether this set is nonempty, identify its boundary, determine its optimal diameter, and characterize what replaces it when n b_n=O(1). Do not use undefined phrases such as “honest interval,” “rate-optimal diameter,” or “the bandwidth region.” Fifth, add Ai, Huang, and Zhang (2026), Data-Driven Uniform Inference for General Continuous Treatment Models via Minimum-Variance Weighting, to the bibliography, related work, and comparator table, accurately crediting its uniform local-linear/Bahadur and weighted-bootstrap bands with data-driven bias or weighting control for ordinary continuous-treatment response processes, and state precisely that it neither treats a CAR-censored illness-death EIF with learned multistate hazards nor constructs the bounded-effective-count marked compound-Poisson experiment. Finally, preserve all settled v4 repairs and do not revive any burned v1–v3 construction: no non-observed-law point target, signed local-polynomial “intervention density,” unsupported published-EIF substitution, circular Gaussian predicate, score/estimator standard-error confusion, malformed g-formula that fails to suppress pre-u initiation or omits initiation-time-dependent post-treatment and censoring objects, arbitrary kernel rule misrepresented as the published fixed-window intervention, alpha_02-only bump with the wrong separation order, null-event consistency, window crossing t_0, positive “actual bias” for a constant response, zero-variance inference class, or unnamed Gaussian focal frontier. Keep every Gaussian, compound-Poisson, efficiency, honesty, and optimal-diameter conclusion explicitly open until its stated construction and lower bound are proved.

Preserve both open kernels and make only these repairs: define, for each observed law P, the nonempty set of compatible full-data extensions P_full having observed margin P and satisfying consistency, observed-hazard causal validity, and CAR; define both model classes using the existential compatible-extension condition; add an invariance lemma stating that every compatible extension yields P_full(T^u≤t_0)=Psi_u(P) for f_{U,P}-almost every u and therefore the same Hölder-selected theta(P)=g_P(u_star); rewrite target_estimand and all causal headlines with this quantified, extension-invariant meaning. Retag the no-censoring condition alpha_C0,P=alpha_C1,P=0 as an uncited constructed submodel restriction and remove the unsupported Kennedy–Balakrishnan–Robins–Wasserman attribution. Do not add a primitive Gaussian theorem, a bandwidth theorem, a concrete modulus exponent, new claims, or broader assumptions.

Define a bandwidth-sequence-specific C^{BA}_{n,alpha}[b_n], or require 0 in C and b(0)=b_n, then use that same procedure consistently in B^G_alpha and its diameter objective. Do not add primitive-Gaussian or modulus strengthening, new assumptions, or new claims.
Replace the X-dependent EIF citation with Martinussen--Knudsen--Rytgaard Theorem 4.1 and identify Theorem 3.1 only as its no-covariate building block. Make no other revisions and add no claims.

Start a fresh angle that independently derives the EIF for the exogenous at-risk initiation hazard a_b=q_b/Qbar_b, treating Martinussen--Knudsen--Rytgaard only as informed-by prior art and never as licensing q_b substitution. Explicitly define a nonempty triangular law sequence, the exact centered/scaled statistic, and the Laplace-functional map from the limiting marked point process to the real-valued critical law. Preserve the field-tier effective-count/honest-inference topic, but do not reuse any angle-0 construction that depends on the false Theorem 4.1 substitution.

Wholesale-rebuild the mathematical substrate from first principles without invoking or substituting Martinussen–Knudsen–Rytgaard Theorem 4.1: define \(a_b=q_b/\bar Q_b\) only on \(\{\bar Q_b>0\}\), set \(S_0^b(t\mid x)=\bar Q_b(t)e^{-A_{02}(t\mid x)}\), use the flux \(S_0^ba_b=q_be^{-A_{02}}\), and declare the untreated state zero/absorbed into the post-initiation state once \(\bar Q_b=0\). Replace marginal overlap by conditional positivity on every \(P_X\)-stratum reached by \(q_b\), together with censoring positivity and the exact inverse-hazard/censoring \(L_2\) integrability needed for the score. Independently pathwise-differentiate \(R_b\), introducing \(V_1(t,u,x)=1-e^{-\int_t^{t_0}\alpha_{12}(r\mid u,x)dr}\); correct \(W_1\) to include \(G_0(u\mid x)G_1(t,u\mid x)\), verify all martingale contrasts and the uncensored \(a_b=\alpha_{01}\) reduction, and retain `thm:exogenous-eif` only if that derivation proves the displayed score. Then formulate the critical experiment in the required order: triangular law; oracle centered score statistic with deterministic variance normalization; explicit conditional mark kernel and Laplace-functional convergence of the localized point process; continuous-mapping compound-Poisson limit and atom analysis; only afterward a precisely defined empirical self-normalized map, including its zero-local-count/zero-denominator fallback, plus cross-fitting nuisance rates, uniform integrability, and remainder conditions proving plug-in equivalence. Keep Gaussian, critical-law, coverage, and minimax-diameter conclusions labeled open until proved; do not assume them or strengthen overlap/smoothness so far that the bounded-effective-count target disappears.

Add ass:conditional-initiation-positivity to lem:extension-invariance’s statement, depends_on, and assumption used_by, explicitly invoking local full support of \(f_{U,P}\) for Hölder-version uniqueness; replace every theorem-number attribution to Martinussen–Knudsen–Rytgaard with “Sections 3.1 and 4 derive EIFs solely for their own natural-hazard-dependent normalized-window intervention; the externally fixed-\(q_b\) EIF here is independently pathwise differentiated and is not obtained by substitution”; leave the displayed EIF unchanged and require its proof to establish the \(G_0G_1\) post-initiation weight, time-dependent \(V_1\), centering, and stated uncensored reduction.

1. Type score integrability only for the fixed \(b\) EIF theorem. Use the actual bracket condition
\[
E_P\!\left[(V_{0,P,b}(0,X)-R_b(P))^2+
\int [W_{0,P,b}(1-V_{0,P,b})]^2\,d\langle M_{02,P}\rangle+
\int [W_{1,P,b}(1-V_{1,P})]^2\,d\langle M_{12,P}\rangle\right]<\infty .
\]
This includes the relevant predictable risk indicators through the martingale brackets. Remove the free-\(b\) assumption from the estimator-independent minimax class \(\mathcal P_s(L)\). For \(b_n\), separately derive triangular-array variance, Lindeberg/uniform-integrability, nuisance-rate, and remainder conditions. Do not encode \(b_n\), the fitted estimator, or uniform inference into \(\mathcal P_s(L)\); honesty and nonemptiness of \(\mathcal B_\alpha^G\) remain questions to prove.

2. Add `ass:bounded-hazards` to `lem:extension-invariance` and its dependency list, and define the relevant marginal initiation density explicitly. In the stated baseline-eligible hazard model,
\[
f_U(u)=E\!\left[S^o_{0,P}(u\mid X)\alpha_{01,P}(u\mid X)\right],
\]
or, for observed uncensored initiation,
\[
f_U^{\rm obs}(u)=E\!\left[S^o_{0,P}(u\mid X)G_{0,P}(u\mid X)\alpha_{01,P}(u\mid X)\right].
\]
Bounded hazards give \(S^o_0\ge e^{-2H\tau}\) and \(G_0\ge e^{-H\tau}\). With \(m(X)=\inf_{|u-u_\star|\le\delta}\alpha_{01,P}(u\mid X)>0\) almost surely, the respective density is bounded below by \(e^{-2H\tau}E[m(X)]>0\), or \(e^{-3H\tau}E[m(X)]>0\). Thus local full support follows. If baseline eligibility is not guaranteed, additionally require positive baseline eligibility in the relevant strata; bounded hazards cannot create support from an empty initial risk set.

3. Replace the global uncensored claim by its active-support form. Let
\[
A_0=\{t:\overline Q_b(t)>0\},\qquad
A_1=\{(t,u):q_b(u)>0,\ \overline Q_b(u)>0,\ u\le t\le t_0\}.
\]
If \(\alpha_{01,P}=a_b\) on \(A_0\) and censoring hazards vanish, then
\[
W_{0,P,b}=1\ \text{on }A_0,\qquad W_{1,P,b}=1\ \text{on }A_1,
\]
while both weights—and hence their martingale integrands—are zero off their respective active supports under the adopted convention. Equivalently, the globally defined weights reduce to the appropriate active-support indicators, not to the constant one.

FINDING: The three flags are distinct, repairable hygiene/well-posedness defects, not a recurring same-root failure. They concern three separate interfaces: full-data censoring identification, endpoint typing of the post-initiation EIF weight, and separation of asymptotic regimes. No terminal/cap action is warranted.

ACTION: REVISE and retain for convergence. Replace `ass:car` by genuine CAR: if \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{full}}_{t-}\) is the full-data filtration and \(\mathcal H_{t-}\subseteq\mathcal F^{\mathrm{full}}_{t-}\) the observed-history filtration, require
\[
\lambda_C^{\mathrm{full}}(t\mid\mathcal F^{\mathrm{full}}_{t-})
=\lambda_C^{\mathrm{obs}}(t\mid\mathcal H_{t-})
\quad dt\times P_{\mathrm{full}}\text{-a.e.},
\]
for an \(\mathcal H_{t-}\)-predictable intensity \(\lambda_C^{\mathrm{obs}}\); define the pre/post-initiation censoring hazards and \(G_0,G_1\) from that observed-history intensity. Do not restate equality after conditioning both sides on \(\mathcal H_t\). Define
\[
W_{1,P,b}(t,u,x)=
\frac{S^b_{0,P}(u\mid x)a_b(u)}
{S^o_{0,P}(u\mid x)\alpha_{01,P}(u\mid x)G_{0,P}(u\mid x)G_{1,P}(t,u\mid x)}
\]
only on \(A_1=\{(t,u):q_b(u)>0,\ \overline Q_b(u)>0,\ u\le t\le t_0\}\), and set \(W_1=0\) off \(A_1\), including exhaustion endpoints; likewise set every inactive or endpoint martingale integrand to zero. Add a separate diverging-count triangular family \(P_G^{(n)}\) with \(b_n\downarrow0\) and \(nb_n\to\infty\), and define its oracle statistic
\[
Z^o_{G,n}
=
\frac{\widetilde R^{\,G}_{b_n}-R_{b_n}(P_G^{(n)})}
{s^o_{G,n}},
\qquad
s^o_{G,n}
=
\sqrt{\frac{v_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}}{n}},
\]
where
\[
\widetilde R^{\,G}_{b_n}
=
R_{b_n}(P_G^{(n)})
+\frac1n\sum_{i=1}^n\phi^q_{P_G^{(n)},b_n}(O_{ni}).
\]
State Gaussian convergence only as an open problem conditional on an explicit triangular Lindeberg condition, deterministic variance stabilization/nondegeneracy, and separately enumerated cross-fitted nuisance, remainder, survival, and uniform-integrability conditions. Retain \(P^{(n)}=P_0,\ b_n=\lambda/n\) exclusively for the critical marked-Poisson experiment.

DIRECTIVE: Advance after those three literal repairs. Preserve the exogenous EIF as independently pathwise derived. Leave all Gaussian, marked-Poisson, plug-in-equivalence, self-normalization, confidence-honesty, and minimax-diameter conclusions explicitly open; none may be implied by the repaired definitions.
